title: Lug Authoring Workflow -- creating complete, implementable lugs
what: The lug authoring workflow is the process for creating a lug that is complete, unambiguous, and dispatchable by Ozi or any future agent. A lug must be authored with full PEV (perceive/execute/verify), acceptance criteria, effort and impact scores, target_files, and model_fit before it is stored. Incomplete lugs are a primary failure mode.
why: Lugs travel across sessions and agents. If a lug is incomplete when created, the agent dispatching it will have no context. Ozi cannot execute placeholder lugs. Every lug must be implementable at creation.

use_cases.title: Creating an impl lug for a new feature
use_cases.trigger: User describes a new feature or improvement
use_cases.outcome: Agent creates complete impl lug with PEV, acceptance criteria, effort, impact, model_fit, target_files, blocked_by. Stored in bytype/implementation/open/.

use_cases.title: Creating a spec lug for a new behavior
use_cases.trigger: A new spoke feature is implemented
use_cases.outcome: Agent creates spec lug documenting: what, why, audience, use_cases, how, tests. Stored in bytype/spec/active/.

use_cases.title: Lug review before dispatch
use_cases.trigger: Ozi or lug-reviewer subagent audits open lugs
use_cases.outcome: Lugs missing required fields are flagged - agent must complete them before they enter the dispatch queue.

patterns.name: Lug before plan execution
patterns.description: Always create and improve the lug BEFORE starting execution. The lug is the spec; execution follows the lug.

patterns.name: PEV chain for complexity
patterns.description: For complex work (6+ steps or 2+ files), use structured perceive/execute/verify fields rather than free-text description.

how.trigger: Planning gate: any change touching 2+ files or 6+ steps requires a lug before execution
steps_summary: 1. Determine type: implementation (planned work), spec (behavior doc), bug, feature, epic
2. Generate id: sha256(title)[:12] or descriptive slug for impl/spec lugs
3. Set required fields: title, impact, effort, model_fit, routed_to, target_files
4. Write perceive (current state), execute (numbered steps), verify (commands)
constraints: Never create a lug without complete PEV, acceptance criteria, effort score, and file targets
spec_id must reference an existing active spec lug if one covers the behavior
model_fit must be haiku, sonnet, or opus

triggers: Before any implementation of non-trivial work
When planning a new spoke feature
not_when: One-line fixes or pure read-only operations

inputs: User description of work or feature

constraints: Placeholder lugs are banned - Ozi enforces this
Do not walk away from creation without a complete lug
body: A lug is a JSON work item stored at WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/{type}/{status}/{id}.json. The lug authoring workflow defines the minimum viable content required before a lug is stored. Every actionable lug (type: task, bug, feature, implementation, epic, review) must include PEV fields: perceive (what to read before starting), execute (numbered steps to take), and verify (commands that confirm success). Additionally required are: title (5+ words, indicative of intent), impact (1-10), effort (1-5), model_fit (haiku/sonnet/opus), routed_to (LOCAL/FRAMEWORK/SPOKE/{id}/ASSESSOR), and acceptance_criteria (array of testable strings). Implementation lugs also require target_files or files_to_edit so sub-agent dispatch knows where to work. The lug ID is generated as sha256(title)[:12] for standard lugs, or a descriptive slug for foundation, epic, and spec lugs. The planning gate fires when work touches 2+ files or has 6+ steps — a lug must exist before any implementation begins. After authoring, the Navigator recommendations-current.json is consulted to inject recommended_model if available. GT candidacy is evaluated: if model_fit=haiku and gastown is installed, execution_substrate is set to gastown and gt_convoy_hint is derived from the title. The lug-reviewer subagent and Ozi both enforce PEV completeness — lugs missing required fields are rejected at dispatch time. Placeholder lugs (lugs with perceive or execute referencing 'TBD', 'see above', or left empty) are banned. Spec lugs follow a distinct draft/active/deprecated lifecycle rather than open/completed. A lug is only considered dogfood-ready when a cold agent with zero session context could implement it correctly without asking a single clarifying question.
done_when: A lug stored in bytype/*/open/ has non-empty perceive, execute (with numbered steps), and verify fields
model_fit is one of haiku, sonnet, or opus — absent or invalid values cause Ozi to skip the lug
An implementation lug has at least one file path in target_files or files_to_edit
The lug-reviewer subagent rejects a lug with empty acceptance_criteria and returns a list of missing fields
Lug ID matches sha256(title)[:12] for standard lugs or a valid descriptive slug for epic/foundation/spec types
